hypothesis: If open-weight language models trained on alloy compositions are further optimized via Direct Preference Optimization using Thermo-Calc–derived hierarchical preference pairs, then they will generate BCC/B2 superalloy compositions with higher thermodynamic reward (coexistence with BCC-first, room-temperature B2, <10% other phases), greater compositional validity, coverage, and novelty than SFT-only and prompt/agentic baselines because physics-based preference learning aligns the model with thermodynamically feasible regions while mitigating distribution shift.
